title: 歪唇的人
originalTitle: The Man with the Twisted Lip
author: Unknown Author

content.original: The Man with the Twisted Lip
content.translated: 歪唇男人

content.original: Isa Whitney, brother of the late Elias Whitney, D.D., Principal of the Theological College of St. George's, was much addicted to opium. The habit grew upon him, as I understand, from some foolish freak when he was at college; for having read De Quincey's description of his dreams and sensations, he had drenched his tobacco with laudanum in an attempt to produce the same effects. He found, as so many more have done, that the practice is easier to attain than to get rid of, and for many years he continued to be a slave to the drug, an object of mingled horror and pity to his friends and relatives. I can see him now, with yellow, pasty face, drooping lids, and pin-point pupils, all huddled in a chair, the wreck and ruin of a noble man.
content.translated: 伊萨·惠特尼，已故伊莱亚斯·惠特尼博士的弟弟，圣乔治神学院的校长，对鸦片非常上瘾。据我所知，这个习惯是从他在大学时的一次愚蠢的恶作剧开始的；因为他读了德·昆西对他的梦境和感觉的描述后，便试图用鸦片酊浸泡他的烟草，以产生同样的效果。他发现，和许多人一样，这种做法比戒掉更容易，多年来，他一直沉溺于这种毒品，这让他的朋友和亲戚既恐惧又同情。我现在还能看到他，脸色蜡黄，眼皮下垂，瞳孔极小，整个人蜷缩在椅子里，是一个高贵的人的残骸和废墟。

content.original: One night—it was in June, '89—there came a ring to my bell, about the hour when a man gives his first yawn and glances at the clock. I sat up in my chair, and my wife laid her needle-work down in her lap and made a little face of disappointment.
content.translated: 一个晚上——那是1889年六月——大约在一个人打第一个哈欠并看钟点的时候，我的门铃响了。我从椅子上坐直了身子，我的妻子放下手中的针线活，脸上露出了一丝失望的神情。

content.original: “A patient!” said she. “You'll have to go out.”
content.translated: “有个病人！”她说。“你得出去。”

content.original: I groaned, for I was newly come back from a weary day.
content.translated: 我呻吟了一声，因为我刚结束疲惫的一天回来。

content.original: We heard the door open, a few hurried words, and then quick steps upon the linoleum. Our own door flew open, and a lady, clad in some dark-coloured stuff, with a black veil, entered the room.
content.translated: 我们听到门开了，传来几句匆匆忙忙的话语，然后是踩在油毡上的急促脚步声。我们自己的门砰地打开了，一位穿着深色衣物、戴着黑色面纱的女士走进了房间。

content.original: “You will excuse my calling so late,” she began, and then, suddenly losing her self-control, she ran forward, threw her arms about my wife's neck, and sobbed upon her shoulder. “Oh, I'm in such trouble!” she cried; “I do so want a little help.”
content.translated: “请原谅我这么晚才来打扰，”她开了口，然后突然失去了自制力，跑上前去，搂住我妻子的脖子，在她肩上抽泣。“哦，我遇到大麻烦了！”她哭喊道，“我真的非常需要一点帮助。”

content.original: “Why,” said my wife, pulling up her veil, “it is Kate Whitney.  How you startled me, Kate! I had not an idea who you were when you came in.”
content.translated: “天哪，”我妻子说道，拉起她的面纱，“是凯特·惠特尼。你吓我一跳，凯特！你进来的时候我一点也没认出你。”

content.original: “I didn't know what to do, so I came straight to you.” That was always the way. Folk who were in grief came to my wife like birds to a light-house.
content.translated: “我不知道该怎么办，所以直接来找你了。” 一向如此。 那些悲痛的人们像飞蛾扑火般地奔向我的妻子。

content.original: “It was very sweet of you to come. Now, you must have some wine and water, and sit here comfortably and tell us all about it. Or should you rather that I sent James off to bed?”
content.translated: “你能来真是太好了。来，喝点葡萄酒和水，舒舒服服地坐在这里，跟我们说说一切。或者你希望我让詹姆斯去睡觉？”

content.original: “Oh, no, no! I want the doctor's advice and help, too. It's about Isa. He has not been home for two days. I am so frightened about him!”
content.translated: “哦，不，不！我也想要医生的建议和帮助。是关于伊萨的。他已经两天没回家了。我太害怕他出事了！”

content.original: It was not the first time that she had spoken to us of her husband's trouble, to me as a doctor, to my wife as an old friend and school companion. We soothed and comforted her by such words as we could find. Did she know where her husband was? Was it possible that we could bring him back to her?
content.translated: 这已经不是她第一次跟我们说起她丈夫的困境了，对我，是作为一个医生，对我的妻子，是作为一个老朋友和老同学。我们用尽我们所能找到的言语安慰和劝慰她。她知道她的丈夫在哪里吗？我们有可能把他带回到她身边吗？

content.original: It seems that it was. She had the surest information that of late he had, when the fit was on him, made use of an opium den in the farthest east of the City. Hitherto his orgies had always been confined to one day, and he had come back, twitching and shattered, in the evening. But now the spell had been upon him eight-and-forty hours, and he lay there, doubtless among the dregs of the docks, breathing in the poison or sleeping off the effects. There he was to be found, she was sure of it, at the Bar of Gold, in Upper Swandam Lane. But what was she to do? How could she, a young and timid woman, make her way into such a place and pluck her husband out from among the ruffians who surrounded him?
content.translated: 看来确实如此。她确信他最近在犯病的时候，经常去城中最东边的一家鸦片馆。以往，他的狂欢总是局限于一天，晚上就会抽搐着、精疲力竭地回来。但现在，他已经着魔了四十八小时，毫无疑问，他正躺在码头的污泥浊水中，吸食毒品或在昏睡中消磨毒效。她确信，在斯万丹巷上段的黄金酒吧里能找到他。但她该怎么办呢？她，一个年轻胆小的女人，怎么能闯入这样的地方，从包围着他的一群暴徒中把他拉出来呢？

content.original: There was the case, and of course there was but one way out of it. Might I not escort her to this place? And then, as a second thought, why should she come at all? I was Isa Whitney's medical adviser, and as such I had influence over him. I could manage it better if I were alone. I promised her on my word that I would send him home in a cab within two hours if he were indeed at the address which she had given me. And so in ten minutes I had left my armchair and cheery sitting-room behind me, and was speeding eastward in a hansom on a strange errand, as it seemed to me at the time, though the future only could show how strange it was to be.
content.translated: 事情就那样，当然也只有一条路可走。我能不能护送她到那个地方？然后，转念一想，她为什么要来呢？我是伊萨·惠特尼的医生，因此我对他说得上话。如果我单独去处理，也许会更好。我向她保证，如果他真的在她告诉我的那个地址，我保证两个小时内会用出租马车送他回家。就这样，十分钟后，我离开了我的扶手椅和温暖的客厅，坐着一辆出租马车飞速向东驶去，去执行一项陌生的任务，至少当时我是这么认为的，只是未来才能揭示这件事有多么奇怪。

content.original: But there was no great difficulty in the first stage of my adventure. Upper Swandam Lane is a vile alley lurking behind the high wharves which line the north side of the river to the east of London Bridge. Between a slop-shop and a gin-shop, approached by a steep flight of steps leading down to a black gap like the mouth of a cave, I found the den of which I was in search. Ordering my cab to wait, I passed down the steps, worn hollow in the centre by the ceaseless tread of drunken feet; and by the light of a flickering oil-lamp above the door I found the latch and made my way into a long, low room, thick and heavy with the brown opium smoke, and terraced with wooden berths, like the forecastle of an emigrant ship.
content.translated: 但我冒险的第一阶段并没有什么大困难。上斯万丹巷是一条肮脏的小巷，隐藏在伦敦桥以东，沿河岸北侧排列的高大码头之后。在一家旧货店和一家杜松子酒店之间，有一段陡峭的台阶通向一个像洞穴般的黑色缺口，我找到了我要找的窝点。我让我的出租车等候，走下台阶，台阶中央被醉汉们没完没了的脚步磨得凹陷；借着门上方一盏闪烁的油灯的光，我找到了门闩，走进一间又长又矮的房间，房间里充满了浓郁的棕色鸦片烟雾，并排排列着木制床铺，就像一艘移民船的船头。

content.original: Through the gloom one could dimly catch a glimpse of bodies lying in strange fantastic poses, bowed shoulders, bent knees, heads thrown back, and chins pointing upward, with here and there a dark, lack-lustre eye turned upon the newcomer. Out of the black shadows there glimmered little red circles of light, now bright, now faint, as the burning poison waxed or waned in the bowls of the metal pipes. The most lay silent, but some muttered to themselves, and others talked together in a strange, low, monotonous voice, their conversation coming in gushes, and then suddenly tailing off into silence, each mumbling out his own thoughts and paying little heed to the words of his neighbour. At the farther end was a small brazier of burning charcoal, beside which on a three-legged wooden stool there sat a tall, thin old man, with his jaw resting upon his two fists, and his elbows upon his knees, staring into the fire.
content.translated: 在昏暗中，人们隐约可以看到一些躯体以奇异而怪诞的姿势躺着，弓起的肩膀，弯曲的膝盖，向后仰的头，以及朝上的下巴，偶尔还有一两只黑暗、黯淡无光的眼睛转向来者。从黑影中闪烁着一圈圈红色的小光点，时明时暗，那是金属烟管碗里燃烧的毒药正旺盛或衰弱。大多数人静静地躺着，但有些人喃喃自语，另一些人则用一种奇怪、低沉、单调的声音交谈着，他们的谈话一阵阵地涌出，然后突然戛然而止，每个人都嘟囔着自己的想法，很少注意邻居的话语。在更远的一端，有一个燃烧木炭的小火盆，旁边放着一个三条腿的木凳，一个又高又瘦的老人坐在上面，下巴靠在两个拳头上，肘部支在膝盖上，凝视着火焰。

content.original: As I entered, a sallow Malay attendant had hurried up with a pipe for me and a supply of the drug, beckoning me to an empty berth.
content.translated: 我一进去，一个脸色蜡黄的马来侍者就匆匆忙忙地走过来，给我递上一支烟斗和一些毒品，示意我去一个空铺位。

content.original: “Thank you. I have not come to stay,” said I. “There is a friend of mine here, Mr. Isa Whitney, and I wish to speak with him.”
content.translated: “谢谢。我不是来留宿的，”我说，“我有个朋友，伊萨·惠特尼先生在这里，我想和他谈谈。”

content.original: There was a movement and an exclamation from my right, and peering through the gloom, I saw Whitney, pale, haggard, and unkempt, staring out at me.
content.translated: 我的右侧传来一阵动静和惊呼，透过昏暗的光线，我看到惠特尼，脸色苍白，憔悴不堪，蓬头垢面，正盯着我。

content.original: “My God! It's Watson,” said he. He was in a pitiable state of reaction, with every nerve in a twitter. “I say, Watson, what o'clock is it?”
content.translated: “我的天！是华生，” 他说道。他正处于一种可怜的反应状态，每一根神经都在颤动。“我说，华生，现在几点了？”

content.original: “Nearly eleven.”
content.translated: 快十一点了。

content.original: “Of what day?”
content.translated: “哪天的？”

content.original: “Of Friday, June 19th.”
content.translated: 6月19日星期五。

content.original: “Good heavens! I thought it was Wednesday. It is Wednesday. What d'you want to frighten a chap for?” He sank his face onto his arms and began to sob in a high treble key.
content.translated: “天啊！我以为是星期三。还真是星期三。你干嘛吓唬人啊？” 他把脸埋在胳膊里，开始用高音调抽泣起来。

content.original: “I tell you that it is Friday, man. Your wife has been waiting this two days for you. You should be ashamed of yourself!”
content.translated: “我跟你说，今天是星期五，老兄。你老婆都等了你两天了。你应该感到羞愧！”

content.original: “So I am. But you've got mixed, Watson, for I have only been here a few hours, three pipes, four pipes—I forget how many. But I'll go home with you. I wouldn't frighten Kate—poor little Kate. Give me your hand! Have you a cab?”
content.translated: “的确是我。但你搞混了，华生，我才来这儿几个小时，抽了三斗烟，四斗烟——我忘了抽了多少。但我会和你一起回家。我不想吓到凯特——可怜的小凯特。把你的手给我！你有出租车吗？”

content.original: “Yes, I have one waiting.”
content.translated: 是的，我有一个在等候。

content.original: “Then I shall go in it. But I must owe something. Find what I owe, Watson. I am all off colour. I can do nothing for myself.”
content.translated: “那我就进去吧。但我肯定欠了些什么。华生，帮我查查我欠了什么。我脸色很差，什么都做不了。”

content.original: I walked down the narrow passage between the double row of sleepers, holding my breath to keep out the vile, stupefying fumes of the drug, and looking about for the manager. As I passed the tall man who sat by the brazier I felt a sudden pluck at my skirt, and a low voice whispered, “Walk past me, and then look back at me.” The words fell quite distinctly upon my ear. I glanced down. They could only have come from the old man at my side, and yet he sat now as absorbed as ever, very thin, very wrinkled, bent with age, an opium pipe dangling down from between his knees, as though it had dropped in sheer lassitude from his fingers. I took two steps forward and looked back. It took all my self-control to prevent me from breaking out into a cry of astonishment. He had turned his back so that none could see him but I. His form had filled out, his wrinkles were gone, the dull eyes had regained their fire, and there, sitting by the fire and grinning at my surprise, was none other than Sherlock Holmes. He made a slight motion to me to approach him, and instantly, as he turned his face half round to the company once more, subsided into a doddering, loose-lipped senility.
content.translated: 我屏住呼吸，走过两排卧铺之间狭窄的通道，尽量不吸入那令人作呕、麻痹神经的毒气，同时四处寻找经理。当我经过坐在火盆旁的高个男子时，突然感到裙子被轻轻一拉，一个低沉的声音低语道：“从我身边走过去，然后回头看我。” 这句话清晰地传入我的耳中。我向下看去。声音只能来自我身边的老人，但他现在仍然如以往一般专注，非常瘦弱，满是皱纹，佝偻着背，一支鸦片烟斗从他两膝之间垂下，仿佛是因极度疲惫而从他手指间滑落。我向前走了两步，然后回头看去。我用尽了所有的自制力才阻止自己惊叫出声。他转过身，这样除了我之外没有人能看到他。他的体型变得丰满，皱纹消失了，迟钝的眼睛重新焕发了光彩，坐在火边，对我惊讶的神情咧嘴一笑的，不是别人，正是夏洛克·福尔摩斯。他向我微微示意让我走近他，然后立刻，当他再次将脸半转向众人时，又恢复了那副老态龙钟、松弛嘴唇的衰老模样。

content.original: “Holmes!” I whispered, “what on earth are you doing in this den?”
content.translated: “福尔摩斯！”我低声说，“你到底在这贼窝里干什么？”

content.original: “As low as you can,” he answered; “I have excellent ears. If you would have the great kindness to get rid of that sottish friend of yours I should be exceedingly glad to have a little talk with you.”
content.translated: “越低越好，”他回答道，“我的耳朵很灵。如果你能行行好，摆脱你那个愚蠢的朋友，我将非常乐意和你聊聊。”

content.original: “I have a cab outside.”
content.translated: “我在外面叫了辆出租车。”

content.original: “Then pray send him home in it. You may safely trust him, for he appears to be too limp to get into any mischief. I should recommend you also to send a note by the cabman to your wife to say that you have thrown in your lot with me. If you will wait outside, I shall be with you in five minutes.”
content.translated: “那就请用这辆马车送他回家吧。你可以放心地信任他，因为他看起来太虚弱了，没法惹麻烦。我还建议你让车夫带一张便条给你妻子，告诉她你已经选择和我在一起了。如果你能在外面等候，我五分钟后就和你汇合。”

content.original: It was difficult to refuse any of Sherlock Holmes' requests, for they were always so exceedingly definite, and put forward with such a quiet air of mastery. I felt, however, that when Whitney was once confined in the cab my mission was practically accomplished; and for the rest, I could not wish anything better than to be associated with my friend in one of those singular adventures which were the normal condition of his existence. In a few minutes I had written my note, paid Whitney's bill, led him out to the cab, and seen him driven through the darkness. In a very short time a decrepit figure had emerged from the opium den, and I was walking down the street with Sherlock Holmes. For two streets he shuffled along with a bent back and an uncertain foot. Then, glancing quickly round, he straightened himself out and burst into a hearty fit of laughter.
content.translated: 很难拒绝夏洛克·福尔摩斯的任何要求，因为他的要求总是非常明确，而且带着一种平静的掌控一切的气场。不过，我感到一旦惠特尼被关在出租车里，我的任务实际上就完成了；至于剩下的，我最希望的就是能与我的朋友一起参与到那些奇特的冒险中，而这些冒险正是他生活中的常态。几分钟后，我写好了便条，付清了惠特尼的账单，把他带到出租车旁，看着他驶入黑暗之中。很快，一个衰弱的身影从鸦片馆里走了出来，我和夏洛克·福尔摩斯走在街上。他弯着腰，步履蹒跚地走了两条街。然后，他迅速环顾四周，挺直了身子，突然哈哈大笑起来。

content.original: “I suppose, Watson,” said he, “that you imagine that I have added opium-smoking to cocaine injections, and all the other little weaknesses on which you have favoured me with your medical views.”
content.translated: “我想，华生，”他说，“你大概以为我除了注射可卡因之外，又染上了吸食鸦片的恶习，以及其他你曾以你的医学观点对我提出的那些小毛病吧。”

content.original: “I was certainly surprised to find you there.”
content.translated: 我当然很惊讶在那里见到你。

content.original: “But not more so than I to find you.”
content.translated: 但我找到你，也不遑多让。

content.original: “I came to find a friend.”
content.translated: 我来找朋友。

content.original: “And I to find an enemy.”
content.translated: 而我，去寻找敌人。

content.original: “An enemy?”
content.translated: “敌人？”

content.original: “Yes; one of my natural enemies, or, shall I say, my natural prey. Briefly, Watson, I am in the midst of a very remarkable inquiry, and I have hoped to find a clue in the incoherent ramblings of these sots, as I have done before now. Had I been recognised in that den my life would not have been worth an hour's purchase; for I have used it before now for my own purposes, and the rascally Lascar who runs it has sworn to have vengeance upon me. There is a trap-door at the back of that building, near the corner of Paul's Wharf, which could tell some strange tales of what has passed through it upon the moonless nights.”
content.translated: “是的，一个我的天敌，或者，不如说，我的天然猎物。简单来说，华生，我正身处一项非常出色的调查之中，而且我希望能从这些醉鬼语无伦次的胡言乱语中找到线索，就像我以前做过的那样。如果我在那个窝点被认出来，我的命就一文不值了；因为我以前为了自己的目的利用过它，而且经营它的那个卑鄙的拉斯卡尔人已经发誓要报复我。保罗码头拐角附近那栋建筑的后面有一个活板门，在没有月亮的夜晚，通过它发生过很多奇怪的事情，它会讲述一些离奇的故事。”

content.original: “What! You do not mean bodies?”
content.translated: “什么！你不是说尸体？”

content.original: “Ay, bodies, Watson. We should be rich men if we had £1000 for every poor devil who has been done to death in that den. It is the vilest murder-trap on the whole riverside, and I fear that Neville St. Clair has entered it never to leave it more. But our trap should be here.” He put his two forefingers between his teeth and whistled shrilly—a signal which was answered by a similar whistle from the distance, followed shortly by the rattle of wheels and the clink of horses' hoofs.
content.translated: “哎，华生，那么多尸体。如果我们在那贼窝里死去的每个可怜虫都能换来1000英镑，我们就发大财了。那是整个河边最卑鄙的谋杀陷阱，我担心内维尔·圣克莱尔进去之后就再也出不来了。但我们的陷阱应该就在这儿。” 他把两根食指放在嘴里，尖锐地吹了一声口哨——这是一个信号，远处也传来一声类似的口哨回应，紧接着是车轮的嘎嘎声和马蹄的叮当声。

content.original: “Now, Watson,” said Holmes, as a tall dog-cart dashed up through the gloom, throwing out two golden tunnels of yellow light from its side lanterns. “You'll come with me, won't you?”
content.translated: “好了，华生，”福尔摩斯说道，这时一辆高大的狗车疾驰而来，划破夜幕，从侧灯投射出两条金色的光柱。“你和我一起去，好吗？”

content.original: “If I can be of use.”
content.translated: 如果我能帮上忙的话。

content.original: “Oh, a trusty comrade is always of use; and a chronicler still more so. My room at The Cedars is a double-bedded one.”
content.translated: “哦，一个可靠的同伴总是有用的；而一个记录员就更有用了。我在雪松别墅的房间是双人床的。”

content.original: “The Cedars?”
content.translated: “雪松酒店？”

content.original: “Yes; that is Mr. St. Clair's house. I am staying there while I conduct the inquiry.”
content.translated: “是的，那是圣克莱尔先生的房子。我正在那里住，以便进行调查。”

content.original: “Where is it, then?”
content.translated: “那它在哪儿呢？”

content.original: “Near Lee, in Kent. We have a seven-mile drive before us.”
content.translated: 肯特郡的李附近。我们还有七英里的路要走。

content.original: “But I am all in the dark.”
content.translated: 但我一无所知。

content.original: “Of course you are. You'll know all about it presently. Jump up here. All right, John; we shall not need you. Here's half a crown. Look out for me to-morrow, about eleven. Give her her head. So long, then!”
content.translated: “当然是你。你很快就会知道一切的。跳上来。好了，约翰；我们不需要你了。这是两先令六便士。明天大约十一点来找我。放开缰绳让她跑。回头见！”

content.original: He flicked the horse with his whip, and we dashed away through the endless succession of sombre and deserted streets, which widened gradually, until we were flying across a broad balustraded bridge, with the murky river flowing sluggishly beneath us. Beyond lay another dull wilderness of bricks and mortar, its silence broken only by the heavy, regular footfall of the policeman, or the songs and shouts of some belated party of revellers. A dull wrack was drifting slowly across the sky, and a star or two twinkled dimly here and there through the rifts of the clouds. Holmes drove in silence, with his head sunk upon his breast, and the air of a man who is lost in thought, while I sat beside him, curious to learn what this new quest might be which seemed to tax his powers so sorely, and yet afraid to break in upon the current of his thoughts. We had driven several miles, and were beginning to get to the fringe of the belt of suburban villas, when he shook himself, shrugged his shoulders, and lit up his pipe with the air of a man who has satisfied himself that he is acting for the best.
content.translated: 他扬起鞭子抽了一下马，我们便飞驰而去，穿梭在无尽的阴暗寂静的街道中，街道逐渐变宽，直到我们飞奔过一座宽阔的带栏杆的桥，浑浊的河水在桥下缓慢流动。远处是另一片沉闷的砖瓦建筑荒野，寂静只被警察沉重而规律的脚步声，或是某些迟归的狂欢者的歌声和叫喊声打破。一团沉闷的乌云缓慢地飘过天空，一两颗星星透过云层的缝隙，发出微弱的光芒。福尔摩斯沉默地赶着马车，头低垂在胸前，像一个陷入沉思的人，我坐在他旁边，很想知道这新的任务究竟是什么，似乎如此费力地考验着他的能力，但又不敢打断他的思绪。我们已经行驶了几英里，开始到达郊区别墅区的边缘时，他突然清醒过来，耸了耸肩，点燃了烟斗，一副他确信自己正在做最好的事情的样子。

content.original: “You have a grand gift of silence, Watson,” said he. “It makes you quite invaluable as a companion. 'Pon my word, it is a great thing for me to have someone to talk to, for my own thoughts are not over-pleasant. I was wondering what I should say to this dear little woman to-night when she meets me at the door.”
content.translated: “华生，你有一种沉默的伟大天赋，”他说。“这让你成为一位非常宝贵的伴侣。老实说，对我来说，能有一个人可以说话是件很棒的事情，因为我自己的想法并不令人愉快。我一直在想今晚这位可爱的小妇人会在门口迎接我时，我该对她说些什么。”

content.original: “You forget that I know nothing about it.”
content.translated: 你忘了我对这事一无所知。

content.original: “I shall just have time to tell you the facts of the case before we get to Lee. It seems absurdly simple, and yet, somehow I can get nothing to go upon. There's plenty of thread, no doubt, but I can't get the end of it into my hand. Now, I'll state the case clearly and concisely to you, Watson, and maybe you can see a spark where all is dark to me.”
content.translated: “我正好来得及在到达李镇之前把案情告诉你。这案子看起来简直荒谬地简单，然而，不知怎么的，我却无从下手。线索肯定很多，但我就是抓不住头。现在，我会把案情清楚简洁地告诉你，华生，也许你能在我一片漆黑的地方看到一丝火花。”

content.original: “Proceed, then.”
content.translated: “那么，开始吧。”

content.original: “Some years ago—to be definite, in May, 1884—there came to Lee a gentleman, Neville St. Clair by name, who appeared to have plenty of money. He took a large villa, laid out the grounds very nicely, and lived generally in good style. By degrees he made friends in the neighbourhood, and in 1887 he married the daughter of a local brewer, by whom he now has two children. He had no occupation, but was interested in several companies and went into town as a rule in the morning, returning by the 5.14 from Cannon Street every night. Mr. St. Clair is now thirty-seven years of age, is a man of temperate habits, a good husband, a very affectionate father, and a man who is popular with all who know him. I may add that his whole debts at the present moment, as far as we have been able to ascertain, amount to £88 10s., while he has £220 standing to his credit in the Capital and Counties Bank. There is no reason, therefore, to think that money troubles have been weighing upon his mind.
content.translated: “几年前——确切地说，在1884年5月——一位名叫内维尔·圣克莱尔的绅士来到了李。他似乎很有钱，租了一栋大别墅，把花园布置得很漂亮，总的来说生活得相当优越。渐渐地，他与邻居们成为了朋友，并在1887年与当地一位酿酒商的女儿结婚，现在育有两个孩子。他没有职业，但对几家公司感兴趣，通常早上进城，每天晚上乘坐5点14分从佳能街出发的火车回来。圣克莱尔先生今年三十七岁，生活习惯有节制，是一位好丈夫，一位非常慈爱的父亲，也是一位深受所有认识他的人喜爱的人。我可以补充一点，据我们所知，他目前的所有债务总额为88英镑10先令，而在首都和郡银行有220英镑的存款。因此，没有理由认为他一直为钱的事情烦恼。”

content.original: “Last Monday Mr. Neville St. Clair went into town rather earlier than usual, remarking before he started that he had two important commissions to perform, and that he would bring his little boy home a box of bricks. Now, by the merest chance, his wife received a telegram upon this same Monday, very shortly after his departure, to the effect that a small parcel of considerable value which she had been expecting was waiting for her at the offices of the Aberdeen Shipping Company. Now, if you are well up in your London, you will know that the office of the company is in Fresno Street, which branches out of Upper Swandam Lane, where you found me to-night. Mrs. St. Clair had her lunch, started for the City, did some shopping, proceeded to the company's office, got her packet, and found herself at exactly 4.35 walking through Swandam Lane on her way back to the station. Have you followed me so far?”
content.translated: “上周一，内维尔·圣克莱尔先生比平时早些时候进城，临行前他说他有两项重要的委托要办理，并且会给他的小儿子带一盒积木回家。恰好，他的妻子在同一周一，在他离开后不久收到了一份电报，说她一直期待的一件相当有价值的小包裹正在阿伯丁航运公司的办公室等她。如果你对伦敦很熟悉，你就会知道该公司的办公室位于弗雷斯诺街，这条街从上斯万丹巷分出来，也就是你今晚找到我的地方。圣克莱尔夫人吃了午饭，前往市区，买了一些东西，然后去了公司办公室，取了她的包裹，发现自己正好在 4 点 35 分走在斯万丹巷，准备返回车站。你跟上我了吗？”

content.original: “It is very clear.”
content.translated: 非常清楚。

content.original: “If you remember, Monday was an exceedingly hot day, and Mrs. St. Clair walked slowly, glancing about in the hope of seeing a cab, as she did not like the neighbourhood in which she found herself. While she was walking in this way down Swandam Lane, she suddenly heard an ejaculation or cry, and was struck cold to see her husband looking down at her and, as it seemed to her, beckoning to her from a second-floor window. The window was open, and she distinctly saw his face, which she describes as being terribly agitated. He waved his hands frantically to her, and then vanished from the window so suddenly that it seemed to her that he had been plucked back by some irresistible force from behind. One singular point which struck her quick feminine eye was that although he wore some dark coat, such as he had started to town in, he had on neither collar nor necktie.
content.translated: “如果你还记得，星期一是个异常炎热的日子，圣克莱尔太太走得很慢，四处张望，希望能看到一辆出租车，因为她不喜欢自己身处的那个街区。当她以这种方式走在斯旺德姆巷时，突然听到一声惊呼或哭喊，随即她惊恐地看到她的丈夫正从二楼的窗户向下看着她，而且在她看来，他好像是在向她招手。窗户是开着的，她清楚地看到了他的脸，她形容说那张脸非常激动。他疯狂地向她挥舞着双手，然后突然从窗户消失了，在她看来，他似乎是被一股不可抗拒的力量从后面拽了回去。一个引起她敏锐女性目光的奇异之处是，虽然他穿着一件深色的外套，就像他进城时穿的那件一样，但他既没有戴领子也没有系领带。”

content.original: “Convinced that something was amiss with him, she rushed down the steps—for the house was none other than the opium den in which you found me to-night—and running through the front room she attempted to ascend the stairs which led to the first floor. At the foot of the stairs, however, she met this Lascar scoundrel of whom I have spoken, who thrust her back and, aided by a Dane, who acts as assistant there, pushed her out into the street. Filled with the most maddening doubts and fears, she rushed down the lane and, by rare good-fortune, met in Fresno Street a number of constables with an inspector, all on their way to their beat. The inspector and two men accompanied her back, and in spite of the continued resistance of the proprietor, they made their way to the room in which Mr. St. Clair had last been seen. There was no sign of him there. In fact, in the whole of that floor there was no one to be found save a crippled wretch of hideous aspect, who, it seems, made his home there. Both he and the Lascar stoutly swore that no one else had been in the front room during the afternoon. So determined was their denial that the inspector was staggered, and had almost come to believe that Mrs. St. Clair had been deluded when, with a cry, she sprang at a small deal box which lay upon the table and tore the lid from it. Out there fell a cascade of children's bricks. It was the toy which he had promised to bring home.
content.translated: 确信他出了什么事，她冲下台阶——因为这房子不是别处，正是你们今晚发现我的那个鸦片馆——穿过前厅，试图登上通往二楼的楼梯。然而，在楼梯脚下，她遇到了我提到的那个拉斯卡尔恶棍，他把她推了回去，并在一个丹麦人的帮助下，把她推出了街道。她满怀令人发狂的疑虑和恐惧，冲下小巷，幸运地在弗雷斯诺街遇到了一群带着巡官的警察，他们正在前往自己的巡逻区。巡官和两名警员陪她回去，尽管老板不断抵抗，他们还是设法进入了最后一次见到圣克莱尔先生的房间。那里没有他的踪影。事实上，在那一层楼里，除了一个面容丑陋的残疾可怜虫之外，什么人也没找到，据说他就在那里安家。他和拉斯卡尔都坚决发誓，下午前厅里没有其他人。他们的否认如此坚定，以至于巡官震惊了，几乎开始相信圣克莱尔夫人的确被骗了，这时，她尖叫一声，扑向放在桌上的一个小松木盒子，撕开了盖子。从里面倾泻出一堆儿童积木。那是他答应带回家的玩具。

content.original: “This discovery, and the evident confusion which the cripple showed, made the inspector realise that the matter was serious. The rooms were carefully examined, and results all pointed to an abominable crime. The front room was plainly furnished as a sitting-room and led into a small bedroom, which looked out upon the back of one of the wharves. Between the wharf and the bedroom window is a narrow strip, which is dry at low tide but is covered at high tide with at least four and a half feet of water. The bedroom window was a broad one and opened from below. On examination traces of blood were to be seen upon the windowsill, and several scattered drops were visible upon the wooden floor of the bedroom. Thrust away behind a curtain in the front room were all the clothes of Mr. Neville St. Clair, with the exception of his coat. His boots, his socks, his hat, and his watch—all were there. There were no signs of violence upon any of these garments, and there were no other traces of Mr. Neville St. Clair. Out of the window he must apparently have gone for no other exit could be discovered, and the ominous bloodstains upon the sill gave little promise that he could save himself by swimming, for the tide was at its very highest at the moment of the tragedy.
content.translated: 这一发现，以及那个瘸子所表现出的明显慌乱，让巡视员意识到事情的严重性。房间被仔细检查，所有的结果都指向一起令人憎恶的罪行。前厅的陈设很简单，是一个起居室，通向一间小卧室，从卧室可以俯瞰其中一个码头的后面。在码头和卧室窗户之间有一条狭窄的地带，退潮时是干燥的，但涨潮时至少会被四英尺半的水淹没。卧室的窗户很宽，可以从下方打开。经检查，在窗台上可以看到血迹，在卧室的木地板上还可以看到几滴散落的血迹。在前厅的窗帘后面，塞满了内维尔·圣克莱尔先生的所有衣服，除了他的外套。他的靴子、袜子、帽子和手表——都在那里。这些衣服上没有任何暴力的迹象，也没有其他关于内维尔·圣克莱尔先生的踪迹。显然，他一定是出了窗户，因为没有发现其他出口，而窗台上不祥的血迹几乎无法让人相信他可以通过游泳自救，因为悲剧发生时正值涨潮最高点。

content.original: “And now as to the villains who seemed to be immediately implicated in the matter. The Lascar was known to be a man of the vilest antecedents, but as, by Mrs. St. Clair's story, he was known to have been at the foot of the stair within a very few seconds of her husband's appearance at the window, he could hardly have been more than an accessory to the crime. His defence was one of absolute ignorance, and he protested that he had no knowledge as to the doings of Hugh Boone, his lodger, and that he could not account in any way for the presence of the missing gentleman's clothes.
content.translated: 至于那些似乎直接牵涉到此案的恶棍。那个水手恶棍素以劣迹昭彰闻名，但根据圣克莱尔夫人的叙述，他被发现在她丈夫出现在窗前几秒钟前还在楼梯脚下，因此他最多只能是这起罪行的从犯。他的辩护是完全不知情，并声称他对他的房客休·布恩的所作所为一无所知，并且无法解释失踪先生的衣服为何会出现在那里。

content.original: “So much for the Lascar manager. Now for the sinister cripple who lives upon the second floor of the opium den, and who was certainly the last human being whose eyes rested upon Neville St. Clair. His name is Hugh Boone, and his hideous face is one which is familiar to every man who goes much to the City. He is a professional beggar, though in order to avoid the police regulations he pretends to a small trade in wax vestas. Some little distance down Threadneedle Street, upon the left-hand side, there is, as you may have remarked, a small angle in the wall. Here it is that this creature takes his daily seat, cross-legged with his tiny stock of matches on his lap, and as he is a piteous spectacle a small rain of charity descends into the greasy leather cap which lies upon the pavement beside him. I have watched the fellow more than once before ever I thought of making his professional acquaintance, and I have been surprised at the harvest which he has reaped in a short time. His appearance, you see, is so remarkable that no one can pass him without observing him. A shock of orange hair, a pale face disfigured by a horrible scar, which, by its contraction, has turned up the outer edge of his upper lip, a bulldog chin, and a pair of very penetrating dark eyes, which present a singular contrast to the colour of his hair, all mark him out from amid the common crowd of mendicants and so, too, does his wit, for he is ever ready with a reply to any piece of chaff which may be thrown at him by the passers-by. This is the man whom we now learn to have been the lodger at the opium den, and to have been the last man to see the gentleman of whom we are in quest.”
content.translated: “拉斯卡经理的事就说到这里。现在来说说住在鸦片馆二楼的那个阴险的残疾人，他肯定是最后一个亲眼见过内维尔·圣克莱尔的人。他名叫休·布恩，他的丑陋面孔对经常去城市的人来说都非常熟悉。他是一个职业乞丐，但为了规避警察的规定，他假装做一些小小的蜡烛生意。在针线街下游不远处，左手边，你可能已经注意到，墙壁有一个小小的转角。这个家伙每天就在那里盘腿而坐，膝盖上放着他那点微薄的火柴，由于他那副可怜的样子，零星的施舍像小雨一样落进他放在路边那顶油腻的皮帽里。我曾经不止一次地观察过这个人，甚至在他成为我的职业对象之前，我就对他在短时间内所获得的丰收感到惊讶。你看，他的外表非常引人注目，没有人会不注意到他。一头蓬乱的橙色头发，一张被可怕的疤痕毁容的苍白脸庞，疤痕的收缩使他的上唇外缘向上翻起，一张斗牛犬似的下巴，以及一双非常锐利的黑眼睛，与他头发的颜色形成了奇特的对比，所有这些都使他从普通的乞丐人群中脱颖而出。他的机智也是如此，因为他对路人扔给他的任何嘲讽都能迅速做出回应。这个人就是我们现在得知住在鸦片馆里的人，也是最后一个见到我们正在寻找的那位绅士的人。”

content.original: “But a cripple!” said I. “What could he have done single-handed against a man in the prime of life?”
content.translated: “可他是个瘸子呀！”我说，“他一个人怎么能对抗一个身强力壮的人呢？”

content.original: “He is a cripple in the sense that he walks with a limp; but in other respects he appears to be a powerful and well-nurtured man. Surely your medical experience would tell you, Watson, that weakness in one limb is often compensated for by exceptional strength in the others.”
content.translated: “他的确是个跛子，走路一瘸一拐；但在其他方面，他看起来是个强壮且营养良好的人。华生，凭你的医学经验应该能看出，一条腿的缺陷通常会被其他肢体的超常力量所弥补。”

content.original: “Pray continue your narrative.”
content.translated: 请继续你的叙述。

content.original: “Mrs. St. Clair had fainted at the sight of the blood upon the window, and she was escorted home in a cab by the police, as her presence could be of no help to them in their investigations. Inspector Barton, who had charge of the case, made a very careful examination of the premises, but without finding anything which threw any light upon the matter. One mistake had been made in not arresting Boone instantly, as he was allowed some few minutes during which he might have communicated with his friend the Lascar, but this fault was soon remedied, and he was seized and searched, without anything being found which could incriminate him. There were, it is true, some blood-stains upon his right shirt-sleeve, but he pointed to his ring-finger, which had been cut near the nail, and explained that the bleeding came from there, adding that he had been to the window not long before, and that the stains which had been observed there came doubtless from the same source. He denied strenuously having ever seen Mr. Neville St. Clair and swore that the presence of the clothes in his room was as much a mystery to him as to the police. As to Mrs. St. Clair's assertion that she had actually seen her husband at the window, he declared that she must have been either mad or dreaming. He was removed, loudly protesting, to the police-station, while the inspector remained upon the premises in the hope that the ebbing tide might afford some fresh clue.
content.translated: 圣克莱尔夫人看到了窗户上的血迹后昏倒了，由于她的出现对警方的调查没有任何帮助，她被警察用出租车送回了家。负责此案的巴顿探长对现场进行了非常仔细的检查，但没有发现任何能为此事提供线索的东西。一个错误是没有立即逮捕布恩，因为他被允许有几分钟的时间与他的朋友水手拉斯卡尔进行联系，但这个错误很快得到了补救，他被抓获并搜查，但没有发现任何可以指控他的东西。的确，他的右衬衫袖子上有一些血迹，但他指着他那在指甲附近被割伤的无名指，解释说出血来自那里，并补充说他不久前去过窗户边，而且那里观察到的污渍无疑也来自同一来源。他极力否认曾见过内维尔·圣克莱尔先生，并保证说他房间里衣服的存在对他来说和对警察一样是个谜。至于圣克莱尔夫人声称她确实在窗户边看到了她的丈夫，他宣称她一定是疯了或在做梦。他大声抗议着被带到警察局，而探长则留在现场，希望退潮能提供一些新的线索。

content.original: “And it did, though they hardly found upon the mud-bank what they had feared to find. It was Neville St. Clair's coat, and not Neville St. Clair, which lay uncovered as the tide receded. And what do you think they found in the pockets?”
content.translated: “结果确实如此，虽然他们在泥滩上并没有找到他们所担心的东西。随着潮水退去，露出来的是内维尔·圣克莱尔的外套，而不是内维尔·圣克莱尔本人。你们猜猜他们在口袋里发现了什么？”

content.original: “I cannot imagine.”
content.translated: 我无法想象。

content.original: “No, I don't think you would guess. Every pocket stuffed with pennies and half-pennies—421 pennies and 270 half-pennies. It was no wonder that it had not been swept away by the tide. But a human body is a different matter. There is a fierce eddy between the wharf and the house. It seemed likely enough that the weighted coat had remained when the stripped body had been sucked away into the river.”
content.translated: “不，我觉得你猜不到。每个口袋都塞满了便士和半便士——421个便士和270个半便士。难怪它没有被潮水冲走。但人体就不同了。码头和房子之间有一股猛烈的漩涡。看起来很可能，带重量的外套留了下来，而剥离的尸体已经被吸入河里。”

content.original: “But I understand that all the other clothes were found in the room. Would the body be dressed in a coat alone?”
content.translated: “但我明白其他所有的衣服都在房间里找到了。尸体难道只会穿着一件外套吗？”

content.original: “No, sir, but the facts might be met speciously enough. Suppose that this man Boone had thrust Neville St. Clair through the window, there is no human eye which could have seen the deed. What would he do then? It would of course instantly strike him that he must get rid of the tell-tale garments. He would seize the coat, then, and be in the act of throwing it out, when it would occur to him that it would swim and not sink. He has little time, for he has heard the scuffle downstairs when the wife tried to force her way up, and perhaps he has already heard from his Lascar confederate that the police are hurrying up the street. There is not an instant to be lost. He rushes to some secret hoard, where he has accumulated the fruits of his beggary, and he stuffs all the coins upon which he can lay his hands into the pockets to make sure of the coat's sinking. He throws it out, and would have done the same with the other garments had not he heard the rush of steps below, and only just had time to close the window when the police appeared.”
content.translated: “不，先生，但事实或许可以被似是而非地解释。假设这个人布恩将内维尔·圣克莱尔推下了窗户，那么没有任何人能亲眼目睹这一罪行。他会怎么做呢？他当然会立刻想到必须处理掉那些暴露真相的衣服。他会抓住外套，正要扔出去的时候，他会想到外套会漂浮而不会下沉。他时间不多了，因为他听到了楼下妻子试图强行上楼的争斗声，也许他的拉斯卡尔同伙已经告诉他警察正急匆匆地赶来。片刻都不能耽误了。他冲向某个秘密储藏处，那里积攒着他乞讨的成果，他将所有能找到的硬币塞进外套口袋里，以确保外套会沉入水底。他把它扔了出去，如果不是听到楼下急促的脚步声，他也会同样处理掉其他衣服，他只是勉强及时地关上窗户，警察就出现了。”

content.original: “It certainly sounds feasible.”
content.translated: 听起来确实可行。

content.original: “Well, we will take it as a working hypothesis for want of a better. Boone, as I have told you, was arrested and taken to the station, but it could not be shown that there had ever before been anything against him. He had for years been known as a professional beggar, but his life appeared to have been a very quiet and innocent one. There the matter stands at present, and the questions which have to be solved—what Neville St. Clair was doing in the opium den, what happened to him when there, where is he now, and what Hugh Boone had to do with his disappearance—are all as far from a solution as ever. I confess that I cannot recall any case within my experience which looked at the first glance so simple and yet which presented such difficulties.”
content.translated: “好吧，在没有更好的说法之前，我们就把它当作一个工作假设。正如我告诉你的，布恩被逮捕并带到了警察局，但无法证明他以前有任何犯罪记录。多年来，他一直以职业乞丐的身份为人所知，但他的生活似乎非常平静和清白。事情目前就是这样，需要解决的问题是——内维尔·圣克莱尔在鸦片馆里做什么，他在那里发生了什么事，他现在在哪里，以及休·布恩与他的失踪有什么关系——所有这些问题都和以往一样，离解决还很遥远。我承认，在我经历过的所有案件中，我记不起有哪个案件乍一看如此简单，却又如此困难。”

content.original: While Sherlock Holmes had been detailing this singular series of events, we had been whirling through the outskirts of the great town until the last straggling houses had been left behind, and we rattled along with a country hedge upon either side of us. Just as he finished, however, we drove through two scattered villages, where a few lights still glimmered in the windows.
content.translated: 当夏洛克·福尔摩斯详细叙述这一系列奇特事件时，我们已经飞速穿过大城镇的郊区，直到最后一排零星的房屋被抛在身后，我们沿着乡村的树篱一路颠簸前行。然而，就在他讲完的时候，我们穿过了两个零星分布的村庄，那里的一些窗户里还闪烁着微弱的光芒。

content.original: “We are on the outskirts of Lee,” said my companion. “We have touched on three English counties in our short drive, starting in Middlesex, passing over an angle of Surrey, and ending in Kent. See that light among the trees? That is The Cedars, and beside that lamp sits a woman whose anxious ears have already, I have little doubt, caught the clink of our horse's feet.”
content.translated: “我们现在位于利镇郊外，”我的同伴说道。“在短短的车程中，我们已经经过了三个英国郡，从米德尔塞克斯郡开始，越过萨里郡的一个角，最后到达肯特郡。看到树丛中的那盏灯了吗？那是雪松山庄，在灯旁坐着一位女士，我毫不怀疑，她焦急的耳朵已经捕捉到我们马蹄的叮当声了。”

content.original: “But why are you not conducting the case from Baker Street?” I asked.
content.translated: “那你为什么不在贝克街处理这个案子呢？” 我问道。

content.original: “Because there are many inquiries which must be made out here. Mrs. St. Clair has most kindly put two rooms at my disposal, and you may rest assured that she will have nothing but a welcome for my friend and colleague. I hate to meet her, Watson, when I have no news of her husband. Here we are. Whoa, there, whoa!”
content.translated: “因为这里有很多事情需要调查。圣克莱尔太太非常慷慨地腾出了两间房间给我使用，你可以放心，她一定会欢迎我的朋友和同事。我真不想在没有任何关于她丈夫的消息时与她见面，华生。我们到了。吁，停，吁！”

content.original: We had pulled up in front of a large villa which stood within its own grounds. A stable-boy had run out to the horse's head, and springing down, I followed Holmes up the small, winding gravel-drive which led to the house. As we approached, the door flew open, and a little blonde woman stood in the opening, clad in some sort of light mousseline de soie, with a touch of fluffy pink chiffon at her neck and wrists. She stood with her figure outlined against the flood of light, one hand upon the door, one half-raised in her eagerness, her body slightly bent, her head and face protruded, with eager eyes and parted lips, a standing question.
content.translated: 我们在一栋大型别墅前停了下来，别墅矗立在自己的庭院里。一个马厩男孩跑出来拉住马头，我跳下车，跟着福尔摩斯走上通往房子的蜿蜒碎石小路。当我们走近时，门猛然打开，一个金发小个子女人站在门口，身穿某种轻薄的丝绸，脖子和手腕处点缀着蓬松的粉色雪纺。她站在灯光下，身影清晰可见，一只手扶着门，另一只手半抬起，显得很急切，身体微微前倾，头部和脸部探出，眼睛渴望地望着，嘴唇微微张开，仿佛一个站立的疑问。

content.original: “Well?” she cried, “well?” And then, seeing that there were two of us, she gave a cry of hope which sank into a groan as she saw that my companion shook his head and shrugged his shoulders.
content.translated: “怎么样？”她叫道，“怎么样？” 接着，她看到我们两个人，便发出一声充满希望的叫喊，但当她看到我的同伴摇了摇头，耸了耸肩时，这声叫喊又变成了痛苦的呻吟。

content.original: “No good news?”
content.translated: 没有好消息吗？

content.original: “None.”
“No bad?”
content.translated: “还行？”

content.original: “No.”
content.translated: 不。

content.original: “Thank God for that. But come in. You must be weary, for you have had a long day.”
content.translated: “感谢上帝。快请进，你一定累坏了，今天够你受的。”

content.original: “This is my friend, Dr. Watson. He has been of most vital use to me in several of my cases, and a lucky chance has made it possible for me to bring him out and associate him with this investigation.”
content.translated: 这位是我的朋友，华生医生。在我的几个案子里，他都起到了至关重要的作用，而且幸运的是，这次我能够带他出来，让他参与这次调查。

content.original: “I am delighted to see you,” said she, pressing my hand warmly. “You will, I am sure, forgive anything that may be wanting in our arrangements, when you consider the blow which has come so suddenly upon us.”
content.translated: “我很高兴见到你，”她说，热情地握着我的手。“我相信，当你考虑到这场突如其来的打击时，你会原谅我们安排上任何不足之处的。”

content.original: “My dear madam,” said I, “I am an old campaigner, and if I were not I can very well see that no apology is needed. If I can be of any assistance, either to you or to my friend here, I shall be indeed happy.”
content.translated: “我亲爱的夫人，”我说，“我是一个老兵了，即使不是，我也能很清楚地看到没有任何需要道歉的地方。如果我能为您或者我这位朋友提供任何帮助，我将感到非常荣幸。”

content.original: “Now, Mr. Sherlock Holmes,” said the lady as we entered a well-lit dining-room, upon the table of which a cold supper had been laid out, “I should very much like to ask you one or two plain questions, to which I beg that you will give a plain answer.”
content.translated: “现在，福尔摩斯先生，”当我们走进一间灯火通明的餐厅时，那位女士说道，桌上已经摆好了冷餐，“我很想问你一两个直截了当的问题，我希望你能给出直截了当的回答。”

content.original: “Certainly, madam.”
content.translated: “当然，夫人。”

content.original: “Do not trouble about my feelings. I am not hysterical, nor given to fainting. I simply wish to hear your real, real opinion.”
content.translated: “不必为我的感受担心。我既不歇斯底里，也不容易昏倒。我只是想听听你真正、真正的看法。”

content.original: “Upon what point?”
content.translated: “基于哪一点？”

content.original: “In your heart of hearts, do you think that Neville is alive?”
content.translated: 你扪心自问，你认为纳威还活着吗？

content.original: Sherlock Holmes seemed to be embarrassed by the question. “Frankly, now!” she repeated, standing upon the rug and looking keenly down at him as he leaned back in a basket-chair.
content.translated: 夏洛克·福尔摩斯似乎对这个问题感到尴尬。“坦白地，现在！”她重复道，站在地毯上，目光锐利地俯视着他，而他正向后靠在藤椅里。

content.original: “Frankly, then, madam, I do not.”
content.translated: 坦白地说，夫人，我不这么认为。

content.original: “You think that he is dead?”
content.translated: 你觉得他死了吗？

content.original: “I do.”
content.translated: 我愿意。

content.original: “Murdered?”
content.translated: “被谋杀了？”

content.original: “I don't say that. Perhaps.”
content.translated: 我不这么说。或许吧。

content.original: “And on what day did he meet his death?”
content.translated: “他是在哪一天去世的？”

content.original: “On Monday.”
content.translated: 星期一。

content.original: “Then perhaps, Mr. Holmes, you will be good enough to explain how it is that I have received a letter from him to-day.”
content.translated: “那么，福尔摩斯先生，您是否能解释一下，为什么我今天会收到他寄来的一封信？”

content.original: Sherlock Holmes sprang out of his chair as if he had been galvanised.
content.translated: 夏洛克·福尔摩斯像触电般从椅子上跳了起来。

content.original: “What!” he roared.
content.translated: “什么！”他咆哮道。

content.original: “Yes, to-day.” She stood smiling, holding up a little slip of paper in the air.
content.translated: “是的，就是今天。” 她笑着站在那里，举起一张小纸条。

content.original: “May I see it?”
content.translated: 我可以看看吗？

content.original: “Certainly.”
content.translated: 当然。

content.original: He snatched it from her in his eagerness, and smoothing it out upon the table he drew over the lamp and examined it intently. I had left my chair and was gazing at it over his shoulder. The envelope was a very coarse one and was stamped with the Gravesend postmark and with the date of that very day, or rather of the day before, for it was considerably after midnight.
content.translated: 他急切地从她手中夺过信，将其展平在桌上，拉过台灯，仔细地查看起来。我离开了椅子，站在他身后注视着。信封非常粗糙，盖着格雷夫森德的邮戳，日期就是当天，或者更确切地说，是前一天，因为已经过了午夜很久了。

content.original: “Coarse writing,” murmured Holmes. “Surely this is not your husband's writing, madam.”
content.translated: “笔迹粗糙，”福尔摩斯低语道。“夫人，这肯定不是你丈夫的笔迹。”

content.original: “No, but the enclosure is.”
content.translated: “不，但围墙是。”

content.original: “I perceive also that whoever addressed the envelope had to go and inquire as to the address.”
content.translated: 我也看得出来，写信封的人还得去打听地址。

content.original: “How can you tell that?”
content.translated: “你怎么知道的？”

content.original: “The name, you see, is in perfectly black ink, which has dried itself. The rest is of the greyish colour, which shows that blotting-paper has been used. If it had been written straight off, and then blotted, none would be of a deep black shade. This man has written the name, and there has then been a pause before he wrote the address, which can only mean that he was not familiar with it. It is, of course, a trifle, but there is nothing so important as trifles. Let us now see the letter. Ha!  there has been an enclosure here!”
content.translated: “你看，这个名字是用纯黑的墨水写的，而且已经干透了。其余部分是灰色的，这表明使用了吸墨纸。如果是直接写完然后吸墨，就不会有如此深的黑色。这个人写了名字，然后停顿了一下才写地址，这只能说明他对这个地址并不熟悉。当然，这只是一个小细节，但没有什么比细节更重要了。现在让我们看看这封信。哈！这里面有夹带的东西！”

content.original: “Yes, there was a ring. His signet-ring.”
content.translated: “有，有个戒指。他的印章戒指。”

content.original: “And you are sure that this is your husband's hand?”
content.translated: “你确定这是你丈夫的笔迹吗？”

content.original: “One of his hands.”
content.translated: 他的一只手

content.original: “One?”
content.translated: “一个？”

content.original: “His hand when he wrote hurriedly. It is very unlike his usual writing, and yet I know it well.”
content.translated: 他匆忙写字时的笔迹。非常不像他平时的字迹，但我很熟悉。

content.original: Written in pencil upon the fly-leaf of a book, octavo size, no water-mark. Hum! Posted to-day in Gravesend by a man with a dirty thumb. Ha! And the flap has been gummed, if I am not very much in error, by a person who had been chewing tobacco. And you have no doubt that it is your husband's hand, madam?”
content.translated: 写在八开本，无水印的书的扉页上，用铅笔写的。嗯！今天在格雷夫森德由一个拇指脏兮兮的男人寄出的。哈！如果我没弄错的话，信封的封口是被一个嚼烟草的人用口水粘上的。 您确信这是您丈夫的笔迹吗，夫人？

content.original: “None. Neville wrote those words.”
content.translated: “没有。是纳威写的那些字。”

content.original: “And they were posted to-day at Gravesend. Well, Mrs. St. Clair, the clouds lighten, though I should not venture to say that the danger is over.”
content.translated: “他们今天被张贴在格雷夫森德了。好了，圣克莱尔夫人，乌云消散了，虽然我不敢说危险已经过去了。”

content.original: “But he must be alive, Mr. Holmes.”
content.translated: “但他一定是活着的，福尔摩斯先生。”

content.original: “Unless this is a clever forgery to put us on the wrong scent. The ring, after all, proves nothing. It may have been taken from him.”
content.translated: “除非这是一个巧妙的伪造品，用来迷惑我们。毕竟，戒指并不能证明什么。它可能被从他身上拿走了。”

content.original: “No, no; it is, it is his very own writing!”
content.translated: “不，不；是，是他的亲笔字迹！”

content.original: “Very well. It may, however, have been written on Monday and only posted to-day.”
content.translated: “好吧。不过，它可能是在星期一写的，只是今天才寄出。”

content.original: “That is possible.”
content.translated: 那是有可能的。

content.original: “If so, much may have happened between.”
content.translated: 如果是这样，那期间可能发生了很多事。

content.original: “Oh, you must not discourage me, Mr. Holmes. I know that all is well with him. There is so keen a sympathy between us that I should know if evil came upon him. On the very day that I saw him last he cut himself in the bedroom, and yet I in the dining-room rushed upstairs instantly with the utmost certainty that something had happened. Do you think that I would respond to such a trifle and yet be ignorant of his death?”
content.translated: “哦，你一定不要让我灰心，福尔摩斯先生。我知道他一切都好。我们之间有如此敏锐的感应，如果他遭遇不幸，我应该会知道。就在我最后一次见到他的那天，他在卧室里划伤了自己，而我在餐厅里却立刻冲上楼，确信发生了什么事。你认为我会对这样的小事做出反应，却会不知道他的死讯吗？”

content.original: “I have seen too much not to know that the impression of a woman may be more valuable than the conclusion of an analytical reasoner. And in this letter you certainly have a very strong piece of evidence to corroborate your view. But if your husband is alive and able to write letters, why should he remain away from you?”
content.translated: “我见识得太多了，深知一个女人所产生的印象，可能比一个善于分析推理的人所得出的结论更有价值。而且在这封信里，你确实有一条非常有力的证据来佐证你的观点。但是如果你的丈夫还活着，而且能够写信，他为什么要离开你呢？”

content.original: “I cannot imagine. It is unthinkable.”
content.translated: 我无法想象。这简直是无法想象的。

content.original: “And on Monday he made no remarks before leaving you?”
content.translated: “他星期一离开你之前，什么也没说吗？”

content.original: “No.”
“And you were surprised to see him in Swandam Lane?”
content.translated: “你在斯万丹巷看到他时感到惊讶吗？”

content.original: “Very much so.”
content.translated: 的确如此。

content.original: “Was the window open?”
content.translated: “窗户开着吗？”

content.original: “Yes.”
content.translated: 是的。

content.original: “Then he might have called to you?”
content.translated: “那他可能叫过你？”

content.original: “He might.”
content.translated: 他可能会。

content.original: “He only, as I understand, gave an inarticulate cry?”
content.translated: “据我所知，他只是发出一声含糊不清的叫喊？”

content.original: “Yes.”
content.translated: 是的。

content.original: “A call for help, you thought?”
content.translated: “你觉得，这是求救信号？”

content.original: “Yes. He waved his hands.”
content.translated: “是的。他挥了挥手。”

content.original: “But it might have been a cry of surprise. Astonishment at the unexpected sight of you might cause him to throw up his hands?”
content.translated: “但那也可能是一声惊呼。看到你，突如其来的惊讶可能让他举起双手？”

content.original: “It is possible.”
content.translated: 有可能。

content.original: “And you thought he was pulled back?”
content.translated: “你还以为他被调回去了？”

content.original: “He disappeared so suddenly.”
content.translated: 他消失得太突然了。

content.original: “He might have leaped back. You did not see anyone else in the room?”
content.translated: “他可能跳回去了。你没看到房间里有其他人吗？”

content.original: “No, but this horrible man confessed to having been there, and the Lascar was at the foot of the stairs.”
content.translated: “不，但那个可怕的家伙承认他在那里，而且那个印度水手就在楼梯脚下。”

content.original: “Quite so. Your husband, as far as you could see, had his ordinary clothes on?”
content.translated: “正是如此。就你所见，你丈夫穿着平常的衣服吗？”

content.original: “But without his collar or tie. I distinctly saw his bare throat.”
content.translated: “但是他没戴领子也没系领带。我清楚地看到了他光着的脖子。”

content.original: “Had he ever spoken of Swandam Lane?”
content.translated: 他有没有提到过斯万达姆巷？

content.original: “Never.”
content.translated: 永不。

content.original: “Had he ever showed any signs of having taken opium?”
content.translated: “他有没有表现出任何吸食鸦片的迹象？”

content.original: “Never.”
content.translated: 永不。

content.original: “Thank you, Mrs. St. Clair. Those are the principal points about which I wished to be absolutely clear. We shall now have a little supper and then retire, for we may have a very busy day to-morrow.”
content.translated: “谢谢您，圣克莱尔夫人。这些是我希望完全明确的主要几点。我们现在稍微用些晚餐，然后就休息，因为我们明天可能会非常忙碌。”

content.original: A large and comfortable double-bedded room had been placed at our disposal, and I was quickly between the sheets, for I was weary after my night of adventure. Sherlock Holmes was a man, however, who, when he had an unsolved problem upon his mind, would go for days, and even for a week, without rest, turning it over, rearranging his facts, looking at it from every point of view until he had either fathomed it or convinced himself that his data were insufficient. It was soon evident to me that he was now preparing for an all-night sitting. He took off his coat and waistcoat, put on a large blue dressing-gown, and then wandered about the room collecting pillows from his bed and cushions from the sofa and armchairs. With these he constructed a sort of Eastern divan, upon which he perched himself cross-legged, with an ounce of shag tobacco and a box of matches laid out in front of him. In the dim light of the lamp I saw him sitting there, an old briar pipe between his lips, his eyes fixed vacantly upon the corner of the ceiling, the blue smoke curling up from him, silent, motionless, with the light shining upon his strong-set aquiline features. So he sat as I dropped off to sleep, and so he sat when a sudden ejaculation caused me to wake up, and I found the summer sun shining into the apartment. The pipe was still between his lips, the smoke still curled upward, and the room was full of a dense tobacco haze, but nothing remained of the heap of shag which I had seen upon the previous night.
content.translated: 我们被安排住在一个宽敞舒适的双人房里，我很快就钻进了被窝，因为经历了昨夜的冒险，我已疲惫不堪。然而，夏洛克·福尔摩斯就是这样一个人，一旦脑海中有一个未解的问题，他会连续几天甚至一周不休息，反复思考，重新整理事实，从各个角度审视它，直到他要么弄清了真相，要么确信他的数据不足以解决问题。我很快就发现，他现在正准备通宵研究。他脱下外套和马甲，穿上一件宽大的蓝色睡袍，然后在房间里四处游荡，从床上收集枕头，从沙发和扶手椅上收集靠垫。他用这些东西搭建了一个类似东方长榻的东西，然后盘腿坐在上面，面前放着一盎司切丝烟草和一盒火柴。在昏暗的灯光下，我看到他坐在那里，嘴里叼着一支老石楠烟斗，眼睛茫然地盯着天花板的角落，蓝色的烟雾从他身上袅袅升起，沉默不语，一动不动，灯光照在他轮廓分明的鹰钩鼻脸上。当我入睡时，他就是这样坐着的，当我被一声突然的惊叹声惊醒时，他仍然是这样坐着的，我发现夏日的阳光已经照进了房间。烟斗仍然叼在他的嘴里，烟雾仍然向上盘旋，房间里充满了浓厚的烟雾，但昨晚我看到的成堆的切丝烟草已经不见了。

content.original: “Awake, Watson?” he asked.
content.translated: “醒了吗，华生？”他问。

content.original: “Yes.”
content.translated: 是。

content.original: “Game for a morning drive?”
content.translated: “早上兜风，怎么样？”

content.original: “Certainly.”
content.translated: 当然。

content.original: “Then dress. No one is stirring yet, but I know where the stable-boy sleeps, and we shall soon have the trap out.” He chuckled to himself as he spoke, his eyes twinkled, and he seemed a different man to the sombre thinker of the previous night.
content.translated: “那就穿好衣服。现在还没人起来，但我知道马厩小厮睡在哪里，我们很快就能把马车弄出来。” 他一边说一边自顾自地轻声笑着，眼睛闪烁着光芒，与昨晚那个忧郁的思考者判若两人。

content.original: As I dressed I glanced at my watch. It was no wonder that no one was stirring. It was twenty-five minutes past four. I had hardly finished when Holmes returned with the news that the boy was putting in the horse.
content.translated: 我穿好衣服时，瞥了一眼手表。难怪没人走动。现在是四点二十五分。我刚穿好，福尔摩斯就回来了，说那个男孩正在套马。

content.original: “I want to test a little theory of mine,” said he, pulling on his boots. “I think, Watson, that you are now standing in the presence of one of the most absolute fools in Europe. I deserve to be kicked from here to Charing Cross. But I think I have the key of the affair now.”
content.translated: “我想验证一下我的一个小理论，”他一边说，一边穿上靴子。“我想，华生，你现在正站在欧洲最蠢的人之一的面前。我活该从这里被踢到查令十字。但我认为我现在掌握了这件事的关键。”

content.original: “And where is it?” I asked, smiling.
content.translated: “那它在哪儿呢？”我笑着问道。

content.original: “In the bathroom,” he answered. “Oh, yes, I am not joking,” he continued, seeing my look of incredulity. “I have just been there, and I have taken it out, and I have got it in this Gladstone bag. Come on, my boy, and we shall see whether it will not fit the lock.”
content.translated: “在浴室里，”他回答道。“哦，是的，我不是开玩笑的，”他继续说道，看到我一脸难以置信的表情。“我刚从那里出来，把它取出来了，现在就放在这个旅行包里。来吧，我的孩子，我们看看它能不能打开那把锁。”

content.original: We made our way downstairs as quietly as possible, and out into the bright morning sunshine. In the road stood our horse and trap, with the half-clad stable-boy waiting at the head. We both sprang in, and away we dashed down the London Road. A few country carts were stirring, bearing in vegetables to the metropolis, but the lines of villas on either side were as silent and lifeless as some city in a dream.
content.translated: 我们尽可能安静地走下楼，来到明媚的晨光中。路边停着我们的马车，衣衫不整的马厩男孩正牵着马。我们两人跳上马车，便飞驰在伦敦路上。有几辆乡下马车开始行动，将蔬菜运往大都市，但两旁一排排别墅却像梦中的城市一样，寂静而毫无生气。

content.original: “It has been in some points a singular case,” said Holmes, flicking the horse on into a gallop. “I confess that I have been as blind as a mole, but it is better to learn wisdom late than never to learn it at all.”
content.translated: “这在某些方面是个独特的案子，”福尔摩斯说着，鞭策马匹疾驰起来。“我承认我之前简直像只瞎了眼的鼹鼠，但亡羊补牢，为时未晚。”

content.original: In town the earliest risers were just beginning to look sleepily from their windows as we drove through the streets of the Surrey side. Passing down the Waterloo Bridge Road we crossed over the river, and dashing up Wellington Street wheeled sharply to the right and found ourselves in Bow Street. Sherlock Holmes was well known to the force, and the two constables at the door saluted him. One of them held the horse's head while the other led us in.
content.translated: 当我们驾车穿过萨里郡街道时，镇上最早起床的人们才刚刚睡眼惺忪地从窗户向外张望。我们沿着滑铁卢桥路行驶，穿过河流，猛冲上惠灵顿街，然后急转弯向右，就到了弓街。夏洛克·福尔摩斯在警界非常有名，门口的两位警察向他敬礼。其中一人拉住马头，另一人领我们进去。

content.original: “Who is on duty?” asked Holmes.
content.translated: “谁值班？”福尔摩斯问道。

content.original: “Inspector Bradstreet, sir.”
content.translated: “布拉德斯特里特探长，先生。”

content.original: “Ah, Bradstreet, how are you?” A tall, stout official had come down the stone-flagged passage, in a peaked cap and frogged jacket. “I wish to have a quiet word with you, Bradstreet.” “Certainly, Mr. Holmes. Step into my room here.” It was a small, office-like room, with a huge ledger upon the table, and a telephone projecting from the wall. The inspector sat down at his desk.
content.translated: “啊，布拉德斯特里特，你好吗？”一位身材高大，体格健壮的官员走下了石板铺成的通道，头戴尖顶帽，身穿青蛙扣上衣。“我想和你单独谈谈，布拉德斯特里特。” “当然，福尔摩斯先生。请进我的房间。” 那是一间小型办公室般的房间，桌上放着一本巨大的分类账，墙上伸出一个电话。 警探在他的办公桌前坐了下来。

content.original: “What can I do for you, Mr. Holmes?”
content.translated: “福尔摩斯先生，我能为您做些什么？”

content.original: “I called about that beggarman, Boone—the one who was charged with being concerned in the disappearance of Mr. Neville St. Clair, of Lee.”
content.translated: “我打电话是想问那个乞丐，布恩——就是那个被指控与李的内维尔·圣克莱尔先生失踪案有关的那个。”

content.original: “Yes. He was brought up and remanded for further inquiries.”
content.translated: 是的。他已被拘捕并还押候审，以作进一步调查。

content.original: “So I heard. You have him here?”
content.translated: “是吗，我听说了。你把他带来了？”

content.original: “In the cells.”
content.translated: 在牢房里。

content.original: “Is he quiet?”
content.translated: 他安静吗？

content.original: “Oh, he gives no trouble. But he is a dirty scoundrel.”
content.translated: “哦，他倒是不惹麻烦。但他是个肮脏的无赖。”

content.original: “Dirty?”
content.translated: “脏吗？”

content.original: “Yes, it is all we can do to make him wash his hands, and his face is as black as a tinker's. Well, when once his case has been settled, he will have a regular prison bath; and I think, if you saw him, you would agree with me that he needed it.”
content.translated: “是啊，我们得费尽力气才能让他洗手，而且他的脸黑得跟补锅匠一样。好吧，一旦他的案子了结，他就能好好在监狱里洗个澡了；我想，如果你见过他，你就会同意我的看法，他确实需要洗个澡。”

content.original: “I should like to see him very much.”
content.translated: 我很想见他。

content.original: “Would you? That is easily done. Come this way. You can leave your bag.”
content.translated: “愿意吗？ 那很简单。这边请。你可以把包放在这里。”

content.original: “No, I think that I'll take it.”
content.translated: “不，我想我还是拿走它吧。”

content.original: “Very good. Come this way, if you please.” He led us down a passage, opened a barred door, passed down a winding stair, and brought us to a whitewashed corridor with a line of doors on each side.
content.translated: “很好。请这边走。” 他领我们走下一条通道，打开一扇带栅栏的门，走下蜿蜒的楼梯，带我们来到一条粉刷成白色的走廊，走廊两侧各有一排门。

content.original: “The third on the right is his,” said the inspector. “Here it is!” He quietly shot back a panel in the upper part of the door and glanced through.
content.translated: “右边第三间是他的，”警督说道。“找到了！” 他悄悄地拉回门上部的一个镶板，朝里面瞥了一眼。

content.original: “He is asleep,” said he. “You can see him very well.”
content.translated: “他睡着了，”他说。“你可以看得很清楚。”

content.original: We both put our eyes to the grating. The prisoner lay with his face towards us, in a very deep sleep, breathing slowly and heavily. He was a middle-sized man, coarsely clad as became his calling, with a coloured shirt protruding through the rent in his tattered coat. He was, as the inspector had said, extremely dirty, but the grime which covered his face could not conceal its repulsive ugliness. A broad wheal from an old scar ran right across it from eye to chin, and by its contraction had turned up one side of the upper lip, so that three teeth were exposed in a perpetual snarl. A shock of very bright red hair grew low over his eyes and forehead.
content.translated: 我们俩都把眼睛贴在栅栏上。囚犯脸朝上躺着，睡得很沉，呼吸缓慢而沉重。他身材中等，穿着粗布衣衫，符合他的身份，一件彩色的衬衫从他破烂外套的裂口中露了出来。正如巡视员所说，他非常肮脏，但他脸上的污垢无法掩盖他令人厌恶的丑陋。一道旧伤疤留下的宽阔伤痕从眼睛到下巴横贯而过，由于伤疤的收缩，他的上唇一侧向上翘起，露出三颗牙齿，仿佛永远在狞笑。一头非常鲜艳的红发低低地盖住了他的眼睛和前额。

content.original: “He's a beauty, isn't he?” said the inspector.
content.translated: “他可真漂亮，不是吗？” 警探说道。

content.original: “He certainly needs a wash,” remarked Holmes. “I had an idea that he might, and I took the liberty of bringing the tools with me.” He opened the Gladstone bag as he spoke, and took out, to my astonishment, a very large bath-sponge.
content.translated: “他确实需要洗一洗，”福尔摩斯评论道。“我料到他可能需要，所以冒昧地把工具带来了。” 说着，他打开了旅行包，令我惊讶的是，他拿出了一块非常大的浴用海绵。

content.original: “He! he! You are a funny one,” chuckled the inspector.
content.translated: “嘿嘿！你真有趣，” 巡视员笑着说。

content.original: “Now, if you will have the great goodness to open that door very quietly, we will soon make him cut a much more respectable figure.”
content.translated: “现在，如果您能大发慈悲，把那扇门轻轻地打开，我们很快就能让他体面得多。”

content.original: “Well, I don't know why not,” said the inspector. “He doesn't look a credit to the Bow Street cells, does he?” He slipped his key into the lock, and we all very quietly entered the cell. The sleeper half turned, and then settled down once more into a deep slumber. Holmes stooped to the water-jug, moistened his sponge, and then rubbed it twice vigorously across and down the prisoner's face.
content.translated: “嗯，我不知道为什么不呢，”警探说道。“他看起来可不像能给弓街警局的牢房增光的人，不是吗？” 他把钥匙插进锁孔，我们都非常安静地走进了牢房。 睡觉的人半转过身，然后又一次沉沉睡去。  Holmes弯下腰，把海绵在水壶里浸湿，然后在囚犯的脸上用力地上下擦了两下。

content.original: “Let me introduce you,” he shouted, “to Mr. Neville St. Clair, of Lee, in the county of Kent.”
content.translated: 他大声喊道：“让我来介绍一下，这位是肯特郡李镇的内维尔·圣克莱先生。”

content.original: Never in my life have I seen such a sight. The man's face peeled off under the sponge like the bark from a tree. Gone was the coarse brown tint! Gone, too, was the horrid scar which had seamed it across, and the twisted lip which had given the repulsive sneer to the face! A twitch brought away the tangled red hair, and there, sitting up in his bed, was a pale, sad-faced, refined-looking man, black-haired and smooth-skinned, rubbing his eyes and staring about him with sleepy bewilderment. Then suddenly realising the exposure, he broke into a scream and threw himself down with his face to the pillow.
content.translated: 我这辈子从未见过如此景象。那人的脸皮在海绵的擦拭下，像树皮一样脱落下来。粗糙的棕色肤色不见了！横贯脸部的可怕疤痕也消失了，那扭曲的嘴唇也不再呈现出令人厌恶的冷笑！一阵抽动扯掉了那蓬乱的红色头发，只见他坐在床上，一个脸色苍白、面容忧伤、外表文雅的男人，黑发柔顺，皮肤光滑，揉着眼睛，带着困倦的茫然四处张望。然后突然意识到自己暴露了，他尖叫一声，将脸埋进枕头。

content.original: “Great heavens!” cried the inspector, “it is, indeed, the missing man. I know him from the photograph.”
content.translated: “天啊！”警探叫道，“的确是失踪的人。我从照片上认出他了。”

content.original: The prisoner turned with the reckless air of a man who abandons himself to his destiny. “Be it so,” said he. “And pray what am I charged with?”
content.translated: 囚犯转过身来，带着一种听天由命的破罐破摔的神情。“好吧，”他说，“那么，我被指控了什么罪名？”

content.original: “With making away with Mr. Neville St.—Oh, come, you can't be charged with that unless they make a case of attempted suicide of it,” said the inspector with a grin. “Well, I have been twenty-seven years in the force, but this really takes the cake.”
content.translated: “关于处理内维尔·圣先生的事——哦，得了吧，除非他们把这事当成自杀未遂来处理，否则你不会因此被起诉的。”警探咧嘴一笑说道。“哎，我在警队干了二十七年，这事儿可真是前所未闻。”

content.original: “If I am Mr. Neville St. Clair, then it is obvious that no crime has been committed, and that, therefore, I am illegally detained.”
content.translated: “如果我是内维尔·圣克莱尔先生，那么很明显没有发生任何犯罪行为，因此，我被非法拘留了。”

content.original: “No crime, but a very great error has been committed,” said Holmes. “You would have done better to have trusted your wife.”
content.translated: “没有犯罪，但犯了一个很大的错误，”福尔摩斯说。“你本应该更信任你的妻子。”

content.original: “It was not the wife; it was the children,” groaned the prisoner. “God help me, I would not have them ashamed of their father. My God! What an exposure! What can I do?”
content.translated: “不是妻子，是孩子们，”囚犯呻吟道。“上帝保佑我，我不想让他们为自己的父亲感到羞耻。我的天啊！多大的丑事！我该怎么办？”

content.original: Sherlock Holmes sat down beside him on the couch and patted him kindly on the shoulder.
content.translated: 夏洛克·福尔摩斯在他身边的沙发上坐下，亲切地拍了拍他的肩膀。

content.original: “If you leave it to a court of law to clear the matter up,” said he, “of course you can hardly avoid publicity. On the other hand, if you convince the police authorities that there is no possible case against you, I do not know that there is any reason that the details should find their way into the papers. Inspector Bradstreet would, I am sure, make notes upon anything which you might tell us and submit it to the proper authorities. The case would then never go into court at all.”
content.translated: “如果你把事情交给法庭来澄清，”他说，“当然就很难避免公开。另一方面，如果你能让警方相信你没有任何嫌疑，我认为就没有理由让细节见报。我相信布拉德斯特里特探长会记录下你告诉我们的任何事情，并提交给有关部门。这样的话，这个案子就根本不会进入法庭了。”

content.original: “God bless you!” cried the prisoner passionately. “I would have endured imprisonment, ay, even execution, rather than have left my miserable secret as a family blot to my children.
content.translated: “上帝保佑你！”囚犯激动地喊道，“我宁愿忍受监禁，甚至处决，也不愿让这个可悲的秘密成为我孩子们的家庭污点。”

content.original: “You are the first who have ever heard my story. My father was a schoolmaster in Chesterfield, where I received an excellent education. I travelled in my youth, took to the stage, and finally became a reporter on an evening paper in London. One day my editor wished to have a series of articles upon begging in the metropolis, and I volunteered to supply them. There was the point from which all my adventures started. It was only by trying begging as an amateur that I could get the facts upon which to base my articles. When an actor I had, of course, learned all the secrets of making up, and had been famous in the green-room for my skill. I took advantage now of my attainments. I painted my face, and to make myself as pitiable as possible I made a good scar and fixed one side of my lip in a twist by the aid of a small slip of flesh-coloured plaster. Then with a red head of hair, and an appropriate dress, I took my station in the business part of the city, ostensibly as a match-seller but really as a beggar. For seven hours I plied my trade, and when I returned home in the evening I found to my surprise that I had received no less than 26s. 4d.
content.translated: “你们是第一个听到我故事的人。我的父亲是切斯特菲尔德的一位教师，我在那里接受了良好的教育。我年轻时曾旅行，后来走上了舞台，最终成为伦敦一家晚报的记者。有一天，我的编辑希望发表一系列关于大都市乞讨的文章，我自告奋勇地提供稿件。这就是我所有冒险开始的地方。只有尝试以业余爱好者的身份乞讨，我才能获得撰写文章所需的事实依据。当我还是演员的时候，我当然学到了所有化妆的秘密，并且在化妆室里因我的技巧而闻名。现在我利用了我的技能。我画了脸，为了让自己尽可能地可怜，我做了一道很长的疤痕，并借助一小片肉色石膏，将一侧的嘴唇固定在一个扭曲的姿势上。然后，我顶着一头红发，穿着合适的服装，在城市的商业区找了个位置，表面上是卖火柴的，但实际上是乞丐。我做了七个小时的生意，晚上回家时，我惊讶地发现我收到了不少于 26 先令 4 便士。”

content.original: “I wrote my articles and thought little more of the matter until, some time later, I backed a bill for a friend and had a writ served upon me for £25. I was at my wit's end where to get the money, but a sudden idea came to me. I begged a fortnight's grace from the creditor, asked for a holiday from my employers, and spent the time in begging in the City under my disguise. In ten days I had the money and had paid the debt.
content.translated: 我写完文章后，便很少再想起这件事，直到过了一段时间，我为一个朋友做担保，结果收到了一张25英镑的传票。我当时不知如何是好，不知道去哪里弄到这笔钱，但突然我灵光一闪。我恳求债权人宽限两周时间，向我的雇主请了假，然后利用这段时间，乔装打扮在城市里乞讨。十天后，我筹到了钱，还清了债务。

content.original: “Well, you can imagine how hard it was to settle down to arduous work at £2 a week when I knew that I could earn as much in a day by smearing my face with a little paint, laying my cap on the ground, and sitting still. It was a long fight between my pride and the money, but the dollars won at last, and I threw up reporting and sat day after day in the corner which I had first chosen, inspiring pity by my ghastly face and filling my pockets with coppers. Only one man knew my secret. He was the keeper of a low den in which I used to lodge in Swandam Lane, where I could every morning emerge as a squalid beggar and in the evenings transform myself into a well-dressed man about town. This fellow, a Lascar, was well paid by me for his rooms, so that I knew that my secret was safe in his possession.
content.translated: “嗯，你能想象，当我明白只要在脸上涂点颜料，把帽子放在地上，静静地坐着，一天就能赚到那么多钱时，要安下心来每周做苦工赚2英镑是多么困难。这是我的自尊和金钱之间的一场漫长的斗争，但最终美元赢了，我放弃了记者工作，日复一日地坐在我最初选择的角落里，用我那张可怕的脸激发人们的同情心，让我的口袋里装满了铜币。只有一个人知道我的秘密。他是我住在斯旺丹巷里的一家低级住所的看守，在那里我每天早上都可以变成一个邋遢的乞丐，晚上把自己变成一个衣着光鲜的城里人。这个家伙，一个拉斯卡人，我付给他很高的房租，所以我知道我的秘密在他那里是安全的。”

content.original: “Well, very soon I found that I was saving considerable sums of money. I do not mean that any beggar in the streets of London could earn £700 a year—which is less than my average takings—but I had exceptional advantages in my power of making up, and also in a facility of repartee, which improved by practice and made me quite a recognised character in the City. All day a stream of pennies, varied by silver, poured in upon me, and it was a very bad day in which I failed to take £2.
content.translated: 很快，我发现自己攒下了一笔可观的钱。我不是说伦敦街头的任何乞丐都能年入700英镑——这还低于我的平均收入——但我拥有一种非凡的优势，那就是我的化妆能力，以及一种机智的口才，这两种能力都通过实践而得到提高，并使我成为这座城市里一个相当有名气的人物。一整天，如流水般的便士，夹杂着银币，涌向我，即使是很糟糕的一天，我至少也能赚到2英镑。

content.original: “As I grew richer I grew more ambitious, took a house in the country, and eventually married, without anyone having a suspicion as to my real occupation. My dear wife knew that I had business in the City. She little knew what.
content.translated: “随着我越来越富有，我的野心也越来越大，在乡下购置了一栋房子，最终结了婚，没有人怀疑我的真实职业。我亲爱的妻子只知道我在城里有生意。她却不知道是什么。”

content.original: “Last Monday I had finished for the day and was dressing in my room above the opium den when I looked out of my window and saw, to my horror and astonishment, that my wife was standing in the street, with her eyes fixed full upon me. I gave a cry of surprise, threw up my arms to cover my face, and, rushing to my confidant, the Lascar, entreated him to prevent anyone from coming up to me. I heard her voice downstairs, but I knew that she could not ascend. Swiftly I threw off my clothes, pulled on those of a beggar, and put on my pigments and wig. Even a wife's eyes could not pierce so complete a disguise. But then it occurred to me that there might be a search in the room, and that the clothes might betray me. I threw open the window, reopening by my violence a small cut which I had inflicted upon myself in the bedroom that morning. Then I seized my coat, which was weighted by the coppers which I had just transferred to it from the leather bag in which I carried my takings. I hurled it out of the window, and it disappeared into the Thames. The other clothes would have followed, but at that moment there was a rush of constables up the stair, and a few minutes after I found, rather, I confess, to my relief, that instead of being identified as Mr. Neville St. Clair, I was arrested as his murderer.
content.translated: “上周一，我结束了一天的工作，正在鸦片馆楼上的房间里穿衣服，当我从窗口向外看时，我惊恐地发现，我的妻子正站在街上，眼睛直勾勾地盯着我。我惊叫一声，举起双臂捂住脸，然后冲到我的知己——那个拉撒路人身边，恳求他阻止任何人上来找我。我听到了她在楼下的声音，但我知道她上不来。我迅速脱掉衣服，换上乞丐的衣服，戴上假发，涂上颜料。即使是妻子的眼睛也无法穿透如此彻底的伪装。但随后我想到，可能会有人搜查房间，而那些衣服可能会暴露我。我打开窗户，因为我的动作剧烈，重新打开了那天早上我在卧室里不小心划伤的一个小伤口。然后我抓起我的外套，外套里装满了刚从我装收入的皮袋里转移到外套里的铜币。我把外套扔出窗外，它消失在了泰晤士河里。其他的衣服本也要跟着被扔出去，但就在这时，一群警察冲上了楼梯，几分钟后，我发现，说实话，我松了一口气，我没有被认出是内维尔·圣克莱尔先生，而是作为他的谋杀犯被捕了。”

content.original: “I do not know that there is anything else for me to explain. I was determined to preserve my disguise as long as possible, and hence my preference for a dirty face. Knowing that my wife would be terribly anxious, I slipped off my ring and confided it to the Lascar at a moment when no constable was watching me, together with a hurried scrawl, telling her that she had no cause to fear.”
content.translated: “我不知道还有什么需要解释的。我决心尽可能地保持我的伪装，因此我宁愿弄脏我的脸。我知道我的妻子会非常焦虑，所以我趁着没有警员注意我的时候，偷偷地摘下戒指，连同匆匆写下的一张字条，一起交给那个拉斯卡人，告诉她不必担心。”

content.original: “That note only reached her yesterday,” said Holmes.
content.translated: “那张便条昨天才送到她手上，”福尔摩斯说。

content.original: “Good God! What a week she must have spent!”
content.translated: “老天！她这周肯定过得太糟糕了！”

content.original: “The police have watched this Lascar,” said Inspector Bradstreet, “and I can quite understand that he might find it difficult to post a letter unobserved. Probably he handed it to some sailor customer of his, who forgot all about it for some days.”
content.translated: 布拉德斯特里特探长说：“警察一直在监视着这个水手。”“我完全可以理解他可能觉得不被注意地寄信是很困难的。也许他把信交给了他的某个水手顾客，而那个顾客过了好几天才想起来。”

content.original: “That was it,” said Holmes, nodding approvingly; “I have no doubt of it. But have you never been prosecuted for begging?”
content.translated: “就是这样了，”福尔摩斯赞许地点点头，“我对此毫无疑问。但是你有没有因为行乞而被起诉过？”

content.original: “Many times; but what was a fine to me?”
content.translated: “很多次了；但对我来说，罚款又算得了什么呢？”

content.original: “It must stop here, however,” said Bradstreet. “If the police are to hush this thing up, there must be no more of Hugh Boone.”
content.translated: “不过，事情必须到此为止。”布拉德斯特里特说道。“如果警方要掩盖此事，就不能再有休·布恩的事情了。”

content.original: “I have sworn it by the most solemn oaths which a man can take.”
content.translated: 我以一个人能立下的最庄严的誓言起誓。

content.original: “In that case I think that it is probable that no further steps may be taken. But if you are found again, then all must come out. I am sure, Mr. Holmes, that we are very much indebted to you for having cleared the matter up. I wish I knew how you reach your results.”
content.translated: “既然如此，我认为很可能不会再采取进一步行动了。但如果你再次被发现，那就必须全部公开。福尔摩斯先生，我确信我们非常感谢您澄清了这件事。我真想知道您是怎么得出您的结论的。”

content.original: “I reached this one,” said my friend, “by sitting upon five pillows and consuming an ounce of shag. I think, Watson, that if we drive to Baker Street we shall just be in time for breakfast.”
content.translated: “我是这样得出的结论，”我的朋友说，“靠着五个枕头，抽了一盎司烟丝。我想，华生，如果我们现在开车去贝克街，应该刚好赶上早餐。”
